c.d: The ability to access and synchronize notes across multiple operating systems (Windows, macOS, Android, iOS, Linux) and devices (desktops, laptops, smartphones, tablets) via native apps or web access.
Features that allow users to structure notes using notebooks, folders, tags, and nested tags. Robust search functionality, including OCR for searching within images and handwritten notes.
An intuitive, clutter-free interface that is easy to navigate and customize. Quick access to essential features, smooth navigation, and a responsive, reliable performance.
Various note-taking tools, including typing, drawing, handwriting recognition, voice recording and transcription, and the ability to add multimedia elements (images, videos, audio files, PDFs).
The ability to share notes with others, collaborate in real-time, assign tasks, and manage team projects within the app.
Consideration of whether the app offers a sustainable free plan or if a paid subscription provides significant additional value through enhanced features, storage, and support.
The degree to which the app allows users to personalize the interface, editor, and note organization to suit individual workflows and preferences, including themes, fonts, and editor settings.
Integration of AI features, such as AI-powered writing assistance, summarization, transcription of voice notes, and intelligent search capabilities.
